mnemonics: When to Take Induction, Think it Through
keyPoints: The Well Ordering Principle states that every non-empty set of integers containing a least element has that element as its least element.
The Well Ordering Principle can be used to prove statements about all positive integers, by proving they hold for 1 and then showing the truth for all other integers.
To use the Well Ordering Principle with mathematical induction, we must ensure that the set of integers we are considering is non-empty and that we have defined a clear 'least' element.
